The stadium itself, as a high-occupancy assembly venue, required full wet pipe suppression under NFPA 13 with standpipe systems sized for fire department operations during maximum occupancy loads. Coordination with the adjacent City of Miami Administration Building ensured shared underground infrastructure was zoned for independent operation during phased project delivery.

narrative: EDITION Residences Miami — Edgewater is a 56-story, 649-ft tower encompassing 750,150 SF of residential and amenity space, with a 13-level integrated parking garage for 971 vehicles. The scope required a compartmentalized hydraulic approach: the parking structure, residential tower, and bayfront amenity deck each operated under distinct hazard classifications and flow demand profiles. Upper-floor western amenity decks with a lap pool and sports fields added an open-area suppression challenge, while the crystalline prismatic form of the building created unique MEP routing constraints through the tower's tapered floor plates. A coordinated riser design ensured system pressure was maintained across 56 floors without exceeding allowable working pressures at ground level.
